meals.title: Breakfast Burritos
meals.description: Make-ahead egg, sausage, and cheese burritos built for the freezer.
meals.body:
## Ingredients
- 6 large flour tortillas
- 10 eggs, beaten
- 1 lb breakfast sausage
- 1 cup shredded cheddar
- 1 diced bell pepper
- 1/2 diced onion
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Hot sauce or salsa, optional

## Instructions
1. Brown the sausage in a skillet, breaking it up. Remove and set aside.
2. Soften the onion and pepper in the sausage fat, ~4 minutes.
3. Pour in the beaten eggs and scramble softly. Fold the sausage back in.
4. Warm the tortillas so they roll without cracking.
5. Fill each with eggs, sausage, and cheese. Fold in the sides and roll tight.
6. Serve now, or wrap individually in foil and freeze.

## Notes
To reheat from frozen: unwrap, microwave 2–2.5 minutes, flipping halfway.

## Tips
- Let the filling cool before rolling if you're freezing — less sogginess.
- A thin layer of cheese against the tortilla acts like glue.

## Variations
- Swap sausage for chorizo, bacon, or black beans (vegetarian).
- Add cooked hash-brown shreds for a heartier burrito.

meals.title: Chicken Alfredo
meals.description: Creamy homemade fettuccine alfredo with seared chicken.
meals.body:
## Ingredients
- 2 boneless skinless chicken breasts
- 8 oz fettuccine
- 2 tbsp butter
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 cup heavy cream
- 1 cup grated parmesan
- Salt, pepper, and Italian seasoning to taste
- Fresh parsley for garnish

## Instructions
1. Season the chicken with salt, pepper, and Italian seasoning.
2. Sear in a hot pan with a little oil, ~5–6 minutes per side, until 165°F. Rest, then slice.
3. Cook the fettuccine in salted water until al dente. Reserve **1/2 cup pasta water** before draining.
4. In the same pan, melt butter and cook the garlic 30 seconds until fragrant.
5. Add cream, bring to a gentle simmer, then whisk in the parmesan until smooth.
6. Loosen the sauce with pasta water as needed, toss in the pasta, and top with sliced chicken.

## Notes
Grate the parmesan fresh — pre-shredded cheese has anti-caking agents that make the sauce grainy.

## Tips
- Don't let the cream boil hard or it can split.
- Add a pinch of nutmeg for a classic alfredo depth.

## Variations
- Swap chicken for shrimp (cook just 2–3 minutes).
- Stir in steamed broccoli or peas for a one-bowl meal.

meals.title: Ground Beef Tacos
meals.description: Weeknight beef tacos with a quick homemade seasoning blend.
meals.body:
## Ingredients
- 1 lb ground beef
- 8 taco shells or small tortillas
- 1 tbsp chili powder
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1/2 tsp garlic powder
- 1/2 tsp paprika
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 cup water
- Toppings: lettuce, tomato, cheese, sour cream, salsa

## Instructions
1. Brown the ground beef over medium-high heat, breaking it into crumbles. Drain excess fat.
2. Stir in chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, paprika, and salt.
3. Add the water and simmer 3–4 minutes until it thickens into a glaze.
4. Warm the shells while the beef finishes.
5. Fill shells with beef and pile on toppings.

## Notes
The splash of water is the trick — it lets the spices bloom and coat the meat evenly.

## Tips
- Toast dry taco shells in a 350°F oven for 3 minutes for extra crunch.
- Double the seasoning batch and jar it for next time.

## Variations
- Use ground turkey or plant-based crumbles.
- Turn leftovers into a taco salad or quesadilla.

meals.title: Peanut Butter Overnight Oats
meals.description: No-cook, make-ahead oats with peanut butter and banana.
meals.body:
## Ingredients
- 1 cup rolled oats
- 1 cup milk of choice
- 1/2 cup plain Greek yogurt
- 2 tbsp peanut butter
- 1 tbsp maple syrup or honey
- 1/2 tsp vanilla
- 1 banana, sliced

## Instructions
1. Stir the oats, milk, yogurt, peanut butter, syrup, and vanilla together in a jar.
2. Fold in half the banana slices.
3. Seal and refrigerate **overnight** (at least 4 hours).
4. In the morning, top with the remaining banana and eat cold.

## Notes
Divide between two jars before chilling for grab-and-go portions.

## Tips
- Too thick? Splash in more milk. Too thin? Add a spoon of oats.
- Warm it 60 seconds in the microwave if you prefer it hot.

## Variations
- Swap peanut butter for almond butter and banana for berries.
- Add chia seeds for extra thickness and omega-3s.

meals.title: Teriyaki Chicken Bowl
meals.description: Sticky teriyaki chicken over rice with steamed veggies — great for meal prep.
meals.body:
## Ingredients
- 1.5 lb boneless chicken thighs, cubed
- 2 cups cooked white or brown rice
- 2 cups broccoli florets
- 1/3 cup soy sauce
- 3 tbsp honey
- 2 tbsp rice vinegar
- 1 tbsp grated ginger
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tsp cornstarch + 1 tbsp water (slurry)
- Sesame seeds and green onion to garnish

## Instructions
1. Whisk soy sauce, honey, vinegar, ginger, and garlic to make the teriyaki sauce.
2. Sear the chicken in a hot pan until browned and cooked through, ~8–10 minutes.
3. Pour in the sauce and simmer 2 minutes.
4. Stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook 1 minute until glossy and thick.
5. Steam the broccoli until crisp-tender.
6. Build bowls: rice, chicken, broccoli. Garnish with sesame seeds and green onion.

## Notes
Thighs stay juicier than breasts for meal prep, but either works.

## Tips
- Portion into 4 containers; keeps 4 days in the fridge.
- Keep sauce slightly loose — it thickens as it cools.

## Variations
- Add edamame, carrots, or snap peas.
- Swap chicken for tofu or salmon.
